concerns.text: Figure 1 (method_pipeline_cropped.pdf) lacks axis labels or scale indicators if it contains quantitative data, or lacks a clear visual legend if it is a schematic. The caption describes the pipeline but does not explicitly map visual elements (e.g., 'OCR Expert', 'QA Generator') to specific shapes or colors in the figure. Ensure all components are labeled directly on the image or via a clear legend for print legibility.

Figure 2 (5_table2_length_distribution_avg.pdf) and Figure 3 (6_short_mix_long_vqa_avg.pdf) are referenced as bar/line charts comparing distributions. The current LaTeX code uses `\includegraphics` without specifying the resolution or ensuring the font size matches the main text. At print scale (single column), the axis labels and legend text in these distribution plots may become illegible. Verify that the source PDFs have vector-based text or high-resolution raster text (>=300 DPI) and that font sizes are >= 8pt.

Figure 4 (7_table8_generalization_niah.pdf) and Figure 5 (8_table10_generalization_video.pdf) summarize generalization results. The captions mention 'Retrieval, Counting, Reasoning' but the figure itself must clearly distinguish these categories (e.g., via grouped bars or distinct colors). Ensure the color palette is colorblind-safe and that the legend is placed within the figure bounds or immediately adjacent to avoid ambiguity when printed in grayscale.

Figure 6 (app_1_doc_pool_topic_distribution_cropped.pdf) in the appendix displays domain distribution. The caption states 'document domain distribution' but the figure must explicitly label the axes (e.g., 'Domain' vs 'Percentage') and include a legend if multiple categories are stacked. Ensure the pie chart or bar chart is large enough to distinguish small slices/categories at print scale.

Figure 7 (2_distribution_panels_min32max49_combined.pdf), Figure 8 (4_distribution_panels_non_even_min50max100.pdf), and Figure 9 (3_distribution_panels_ocr_min32max49_combined.pdf) are subfigures within a single container. The current LaTeX uses ` egin{subfigure}`. Ensure that the individual sub-captions (a, b, c) are legible and that the shared caption clearly explains the relationship between the three distributions. Verify that the x-axis (Token Length) and y-axis (Frequency/Count) labels are present on all subplots or clearly shared.
